verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner (cytoplasmic) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. While it has documented PM residence in lipid rafts and associates functionally with surface receptors (BCR, CD40, CD19), these interactions occur on the cytoplasmic face where its catalytic kinase domain operates. The protein body is not accessible to extracellular binders from the outer leaflet; it faces inward by its own lipid-anchoring mechanism. Inner-leaflet anchoring is the defining architecture, regardless of PM proximity.
